message:
fix(xattr): three audit defects across set_xattr & inline encoder

Multi-pass audit of ffs-xattr surfaced three real defects:

1. set_xattr silently accepted a None external_block when the inode's
   file_acl was non-zero, treating external entries as empty even
   though the inode advertised a real external xattr block on disk.
   The fall-through then wrote a fresh inline copy of the requested
   attribute, leaving the on-disk external value permanently shadowed
   by a divergent inline twin. get_xattr/list_xattrs/remove_xattr all
   already refuse this shape; set_xattr is the missing case.

2. build_inline_ibody returned FfsError::Format("…shorter than 4-byte
   header") when handed a 1..4-byte ibody region with no entries. The
   matching reader, parse_ibody_xattrs, returns Ok(empty) on the same
   geometry, so a roundtrip parse-then-rebuild failed for any inode
   with a sub-header ibody region. Empty entries into a tiny region
   now produce an all-zero buffer; non-empty entries return NoSpace
   (a capacity failure, not a format failure — distinguishable so
   callers can spill to external storage).

3. encode_entries_region wasted up to three bytes of the values
   region per empty-value entry by aligning value_start = (value_tail
   - 0) & !3. Aligning a zero-length slice down to a 4-byte boundary
   has no purpose — there is no value to align — and could produce
   spurious NoSpace errors when packing many empty xattrs. Empty
   values now leave value_tail unchanged.

Three regression tests cover each defect and one prior test was
relaxed to assert the new (and more useful) NoSpace contract for
non-empty entries against a too-small region.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
